Annotation Interface DynamicFormField

@Target(FIELD) @Retention(RUNTIME) @Inherited public @interface DynamicFormField
Marks an entity field as a participant in a dynamically generated input form and carries the metadata required to place and render it. The presence of this annotation is what selects a field for form generation; the actual validation constraints (required, length, range, pattern, …) still come from the standard Jakarta Bean Validation annotations on the same field and are read separately by DynamicModelHelper.

This annotation lets the backend entity remain the single source of truth for an input form: the frontend no longer hard-codes max length, required flags or numeric ranges that the entity already declares.

Dialog membership and ordering (uiOrder())

uiOrder is a comma separated list of dialogId.position tokens. A single token such as "1.3" places the field at position 3 of dialog 1. A field that appears in several dialogs lists several tokens, e.g. "1.4,2.1" = position 4 in dialog 1 and position 1 in dialog 2. Entities with only one form simply use "1.x". The serving endpoint filters by the requested dialog id and sorts by position.

Label (labelKey())

By default the frontend derives the translation key from the field name (HeqF convention, e.g. borrowingRate → BORROWING_RATE). Set labelKey only when an explicit key is required because the derived one would be wrong or conflict (e.g. "CASHACCOUNT_NAME" for an inherited name field).

Inheritance: inherited by subclasses, so a form field declared on a base entity is picked up when generating the form for a subclass.

  • Required Element Summary

    Required Elements
    Modifier and Type
    Required Element
    Description
    String
    uiOrder
    Comma separated list of dialogId.position tokens controlling in which dialog(s) the field appears and at which position.

  • Optional Element Summary

    Optional Elements
    Modifier and Type
    Optional Element
    Description
    int
    fractionLimit
    Maximum number of fraction digits for a numeric input.
    DynamicFormPropertyHelps[]
    helps
    Optional UI hints that influence how the input is rendered (email, password, select options, percentage).
    int
    integerLimit
    Maximum number of integer digits for a numeric input.
    String
    labelKey
    Optional explicit translation key for the field label.

  • Element Details

    • uiOrder

      String uiOrder
      Comma separated list of dialogId.position tokens controlling in which dialog(s) the field appears and at which position. Example: "1.3" or "1.4,2.1".

    • helps

      DynamicFormPropertyHelps[] helps
      Optional UI hints that influence how the input is rendered (email, password, select options, percentage). Mirrors DynamicFormPropertySupport.value().
      Default:
      {}

    • labelKey

      String labelKey
      Optional explicit translation key for the field label. When empty the frontend derives the key from the field name (HeqF convention).
      Default:
      ""

    • integerLimit

      int integerLimit
      Maximum number of integer digits for a numeric input. Use this for floating point fields (Double/Float) where @Digits cannot be used because Hibernate would try to apply a column scale, which is invalid for SQL floating point types. A value of 0 means unset.
      Default:
      0

    • fractionLimit

      int fractionLimit
      Maximum number of fraction digits for a numeric input. See integerLimit(). A value of 0 means unset.
      Default:
      0
